   111	opensession share os://src/git/<remote_b64>/ref/<ref_enc>/path/<path...> --web
   112	```
   113	
   114	`share --web` requires explicit `.opensession/config.toml`.
   115	Git-native writes now target hidden ledger refs (`refs/opensession/branches/<branch_b64url>`); legacy fixed ref writes are removed.
   116	`opensession doctor --fix` installs the shim at `~/.local/share/opensession/bin/opensession` for hook stability.
   117	
   118	## Handoff
   119	
   120	```bash
   121	# Build immutable artifact
   122	opensession handoff build --from os://src/local/<sha256> --pin latest
   123	# -> os://artifact/<sha256>
   124	
   125	# Read artifact payload in desired representation

   130	
   131	# Alias management
   132	opensession handoff artifacts pin latest os://artifact/<sha256>
   133	opensession handoff artifacts unpin latest
   134	
   135	# Removal policy: only unpinned artifacts
   136	opensession handoff artifacts rm os://artifact/<sha256>
   137	```

     4	The public contract is a single Source URI model shared by CLI, Web, and API.

    53

    55	
    56	```bash
    57	# Local source -> remote-shareable source URI
    58	opensession share os://src/local/<sha256> --git --remote origin
    59	
    60	# Optional network side effect

    63

    66	- Required: `--remote <name|url>`
    67	- Default ref: `refs/opensession/branches/<branch_b64url>`
    68	- Default path: `sessions/<sha256>.jsonl`
    69	- `--push` omitted: no network mutation (prints runnable push command)

Chunk ID: 685361

Original token count: 1102
Output:
     6	## Getting Started
     7	
     8	Core principles:
     9	
    10	- One concept, one name.
    11	- One identity, one URI.
    12	- No implicit network mutation.
    13	- Defaults are allowed only when printed in output.
    14	
    15	Beginner 3-step quick start:
    16	
    17	```bash
    18	# 1) Install CLI
    19	cargo install opensession
    20	
    21	# 2) Diagnose local setup (flutter doctor style)
    22	opensession doctor
    23	
    24	# 3) Apply setup after explicit confirmation prompt
    25	opensession doctor --fix
    26	```
    27	
    28	- `doctor --fix` prints a setup plan and asks before applying hook/shim/fanout changes.
    29	- For automation or non-interactive shells, use explicit mode + approval:
    30	  `opensession doctor --fix --yes --fanout-mode hidden_ref`
    31	
    32	Quick path:
    33	
    34	```bash
    35	# 1) Parse agent-native logs into canonical HAIL JSONL
    36	opensession parse --profile codex ./raw-session.jsonl > ./session.hail.jsonl
    37	
    38	# 2) Register canonical session in local object store
    39	opensession register ./session.hail.jsonl
    40	# -> os://src/local/<sha256>
    41	
    42	# 3) Read local canonical bytes back
    43	opensession cat os://src/local/<sha256>
    44	
    45	# 4) Inspect summary metadata
    46	opensession inspect os://src/local/<sha256>
    47	```
    48	
    49	Install:
    50	
    51	```bash
    52	cargo install opensession
    53	```
    54	
    55	Auto-capture note:
    56	
    57	- `opensession` covers parse/register/share/handoff.
    58	- Automatic background capture requires the daemon process (`opensession-daemon run`) to be running.
    59	
    60	Local object storage:
    61	
    62	- In repo: `.opensession/objects/sha256/ab/cd/<hash>.jsonl`
    63	- Outside repo: `~/.local/share/opensession/objects/sha256/ab/cd/<hash>.jsonl`
    64	
    65	Hash policy:
    66	
    67	- SHA-256 of canonical HAIL JSONL bytes.
    68	
    69	## Share via Git
    70	
    71	`register` is local-only. Remote sharing is explicit via `share`.
    72	
    73	```bash
    74	# Local source -> remote-shareable source URI
    75	opensession share os://src/local/<sha256> --git --remote origin
    76	
    77	# Optional network side effect
    78	opensession share os://src/local/<sha256> --git --remote origin --push
    79	```
    80	
    81	`share --git` rules:
    82	
    83	- Required: `--remote <name|url>`
    84	- Default ref: `refs/opensession/branches/<branch_b64url>`
    85	- Default path: `sessions/<sha256>.jsonl`
    86	- `--push` omitted: no network mutation (prints runnable push command)
    87	- Legacy fixed ref `refs/heads/opensession/sessions` is no longer used for new writes.
    88	
    89	Install-and-forget setup:
    90	
    91	```bash
    92	opensession doctor
    93	opensession doctor --fix
    94	# optional explicit mode in interactive shells
    95	opensession doctor --fix --fanout-mode hidden_ref
    96	# automation/non-interactive
    97	opensession doctor --fix --yes --fanout-mode hidden_ref
    98	```
    99	
   100	- `doctor` check mode maps to `setup --check`; `doctor --fix` maps to `setup`.
   101	- `doctor --fix` requires explicit approval: interactive prompt by default, `--yes` for automation.
   102	- Installs/updates OpenSession-managed `pre-push` hook in the current repo.
   103	- Installs/updates OpenSession shim at `~/.local/share/opensession/bin/opensession`.
   104	- On first apply without a configured mode, interactive shells prompt for fanout mode (`hidden_ref` or `git_notes`) and store it in local git config (`opensession.fanout-mode`).
   105	- Non-interactive apply requires explicit fanout mode (`--fanout-mode`) if the repository has no stored `opensession.fanout-mode`.
   106	- `doctor` check output includes daemon status from `~/.config/opensession/daemon.pid`.
   107	- Start daemon with `opensession-daemon run` (or `cargo run -p opensession-daemon -- run` in a source checkout).
   108	- Does **not** modify `remote.<name>.push`.
   109	- Hook fanout push is best-effort and warning-only.
   110	- Set `OPENSESSION_STRICT=1` to fail push when fanout helper is unavailable or fanout push fails.
   111	- PR automation currently targets same-repo PRs only.
   112	- Merge/branch-delete cleanup removes ledger refs immediately; physical object removal follows remote GC policy.
   113	
   114	`share --web` rules:
   115

    41	## Install
    42	
    43	```bash
    44	cargo install opensession
    45	```
    46	
    47	`opensession` is the user-facing CLI. Session auto-capture additionally requires the daemon process to be running.
    48	
    49	## Install-and-Forget Setup
    50	
    51	```bash
    52	# 1) Install CLI
    53	cargo install opensession
    54	
    55	# 2) Diagnose local setup (flutter doctor style)
    56	opensession doctor
    57	
    58	# 3) Apply recommended setup values (explicit confirmation prompt)
    59	opensession doctor --fix
    60	
    61	# Optional: pin fanout storage mode while fixing
    62	opensession doctor --fix --fanout-mode hidden_ref
    63	
    64	# Automation / non-interactive mode
    65	opensession doctor --fix --yes --fanout-mode hidden_ref
    66	```
    67	
    68	`doctor` reuses the existing setup pipeline under the hood (`opensession setup` / `opensession setup --check`).
    69	`doctor --fix` now prints the setup plan and asks for confirmation before applying hook/shim/fanout changes.
    70	On first interactive apply, OpenSession asks which fanout storage mode to use (`hidden_ref` or `git_notes`) and stores the choice in local git config (`.git/config`) as `opensession.fanout-mode`.
    71	In non-interactive mode, `--fix` requires `--yes` and an explicit `--fanout-mode` when no fanout mode is already configured in git.
    72	
    73	Start daemon (required for automatic session capture):
    74	
    75	```bash
    76	# if opensession-daemon binary is available
    77	opensession-daemon run
    78

    41	## 설치
    42	
    43	```bash
    44	cargo install opensession
    45	```
    46	
    47	사용자 표면은 `opensession` CLI입니다. 자동 세션 수집(auto-capture)을 쓰려면 daemon 프로세스가 추가로 실행 중이어야 합니다.
    48	
    49	## Install-and-Forget 설정
    50	
    51	```bash
    52	# 1) CLI 설치
    53	cargo install opensession
    54	
    55	# 2) 로컬 설정 진단 (flutter doctor 스타일)
    56	opensession doctor
    57	
    58	# 3) 권장 설치값 적용 (변경 전 동의 프롬프트)
    59	opensession doctor --fix
    60	
    61	# 선택: fanout 모드를 명시적으로 지정
    62	opensession doctor --fix --fanout-mode hidden_ref
    63	
    64	# 자동화/비대화형(non-TTY)
    65	opensession doctor --fix --yes --fanout-mode hidden_ref
    66	```
    67	
    68	`doctor`는 내부적으로 기존 setup 파이프라인(`opensession setup` / `opensession setup --check`)을 재사용합니다.
    69	`doctor --fix`는 적용 전 setup 계획을 출력하고 동의를 받은 뒤 훅/shim/fanout 변경을 수행합니다.
    70	첫 interactive 적용 시 fanout 저장 모드(`hidden_ref` 또는 `git_notes`)를 선택하며, 선택값은 로컬 git 설정(`.git/config`)의 `opensession.fanout-mode`에 저장됩니다.
    71	비대화형 환경에서는 `--fix`에 `--yes`가 필요하고, 저장된 fanout 모드가 없으면 `--fanout-mode`를 명시해야 합니다.
    72	
    73	자동 수집을 위한 daemon 실행:
    74	
    75	```bash
    76	# opensession-daemon 바이너리가 있는 경우
    77	opensession-daemon run
    78

blocks.text: **Explaining Git hook limitations and options**
**Explaining Git hook limitations and options**

현재는 **“대체 + 체이닝”** 방식입니다. 완전 덮어쓰기만 하는 구조는 아닙니다.

- Git `pre-push`는 엔트리 파일이 1개라, 동일 훅을 여러 개 “추가”로 병렬 설치는 불가합니다.
- 기존 훅이 있으면 백업으로 이동한 뒤(`pre-push.pre-opensession`) opensession 훅을 설치합니다: [hooks.rs](~/opensession/crates/cli/src/hooks.rs:392)
- 설치된 opensession 훅이 실행 시작 시 백업 훅을 먼저 호출해 체이닝합니다: [hooks.rs](~/opensession/crates/cli/src/hooks.rs:58)
- 제거 시 백업 훅을 원복합니다: [hooks.rs](~/opensession/crates/cli/src/hooks.rs:457)

원하면 다음 단계로 “진짜 추가형”도 가능합니다.
1. 기존 훅 파일에 marker 블록을 삽입/삭제하는 `append` 모드
2. `hooks.d` 디스패처 방식(한 파일이 여러 스크립트를 순차 실행)

**Renaming backup files to original**
**Renaming backup files to original**
좋은 포인트라서 바로 반영하겠습니다. 파일명/출력에서 `backup` 표현을 제거하고, 기존 훅 보존 파일을 `original` 의미로 바꿔서 실수 삭제 가능성을 낮추겠습니다. 관련 코드/테스트를 모두 수정 후 훅 게이트까지 다시 검증하겠습니다.

    77	    detected_mode=$("$opsession_bin" setup --print-fanout-mode 2>/dev/null || true)

   302	
   303	fn hooks_dir(repo_root: &Path) -> PathBuf {
   304	    configured_hooks_path(repo_root).unwrap_or_else(|| repo_root.join(".git").join("hooks"))
   305	}
   306	
   307	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   308	// Hook installer
   309	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   310	
   311	/// Install opensession git hooks into a repository.
   312	pub fn install_hooks(repo_root: &Path, hooks: &[HookType]) -> Result<Vec<HookType>> {
   313	    ensure_git_repo(repo_root)?;
   314	    let hooks_dir = hooks_dir(repo_root);
   315	    std::fs::create_dir_all(&hooks_dir)
   316	        .with_context(|| format!("create hooks directory {}", hooks_dir.display()))?;
   317	
   318	    let mut installed = Vec::new();
   319	    for hook_type in hooks {
   320	        let hook_path = hooks_dir.join(hook_type.filename());
   321	
   322	        // Check if there's an existing non-opensession hook
   323	        if hook_path.exists() {
   324	            let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&hook_path)
   325	                .with_context(|| format!("read existing hook {}", hook_path.display()))?;
   326	            if !content.contains(HOOK_MARKER) {
   327	                // Backup existing hook
   328	                let backup_path =
   329	                    hooks_dir.join(format!("{}.pre-opensession", hook_type.filename()));
   330	                std::fs::rename(&hook_path, &backup_path).with_context(|| {
   331	                    format!("backup existing hook to {}", backup_path.display())
   332	                })?;
   333	            }
   334	            // If it contains HOOK_MARKER, we'll just overwrite with new version
   335	        }
   336	
   337	        let template = hook_template(*hook_type);
   338	        std::fs::write(&hook_path, template)
   339	            .with_context(|| format!("write hook {}", hook_path.display()))?;
   340	
   341	        // Make executable on Unix
   342	        #[cfg(unix)]
   343	        {
   344	            use std::os::unix::fs::PermissionsExt;
   345	            let perms = std::fs::Permissions::from_mode(0o755);
   346	            std::fs::set_permissions(&hook_path, perms)
   347	                .with_context(|| format!("chmod hook {}", hook_path.display()))?;
   348	        }
   349	
   350	        installed.push(*hook_type);
   351	    }
   352	
   353	    Ok(installed)
   354	}
   355	
   356	/// Uninstall opensession git hooks from a repository.
   357	pub fn uninstall_hooks(repo_root: &Path, hooks: &[HookType]) -> Result<Vec<HookType>> {
   358	    if !repo_root.join(".git").exists() {
   359	        return Ok(Vec::new());
   360	    }
   361	    let hooks_dir = hooks_dir(repo_root);
   362	    let mut uninstalled = Vec::new();
   363	
   364	    for hook_type in hooks {
   365	        let hook_path = hooks_dir.join(hook_type.filename());
   366	        if !hook_path.exists() {
   367	            continue;
   368	        }
   369	
   370	        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&hook_path).unwrap_or_default();
   371	        if !content.contains(HOOK_MARKER) {
   372	            continue; // Not our hook
   373	        }
   374	
   375	        std::fs::remove_file(&hook_path)?;
   376	
   377	        // Restore backup if exists
   378	        let backup_path = hooks_dir.join(format!("{}.pre-opensession", hook_type.filename()));
   379	        if backup_path.exists() {
   380	            std::fs::rename(&backup_path, &hook_path)?;
   381	        }
   382	
   383	        uninstalled.push(*hook_type);
   384	    }
   385	
   386	    Ok(uninstalled)
   387	}
   388	
   389	/// Check which opensession hooks are installed in a repository.
   390	pub fn list_installed_hooks(repo_root: &Path) -> Vec<HookType> {
   391	    if !repo_root.join(".git").exists() {
   392	        return Vec::new();
   393	    }
   394	    let hooks_dir = hooks_dir(repo_root);
   395	    let mut installed = Vec::new();
   396	
   397	    for hook_type in HookType::all() {
   398	        let hook_path = hooks_dir.join(hook_type.filename());
   399	        if hook_path.exists() {
   400	            if let Ok(content) = std::fs::read_to_string(&hook_path) {
   401	                if content.contains(HOOK_MARKER) {
   402	                    installed.push(*hook_type);
   403	                }
   404	            }
   405	        }
   406	    }
   407	
   408	    installed
   409	}
   410	
   411	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   412	// Secret detection
   413	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   414	
   415	/// A potential secret found in content.
   416	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
   417	pub struct SecretMatch {
   418	    pub pattern_name: String,
   419	    pub line_number: usize,
   420	    pub context: String, // The line with the match (redacted)
   421	}
   422	
   423	/// Default secret patterns: (name, regex).
   424	pub fn default_secret_patterns() -> Vec<(&'static str, &'static str)> {
   425	    vec![
   426	        ("OpenAI API Key", r"sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}"),
   427	        ("GitHub PAT", r"ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}"),
   428	        ("GitHub OAuth Token", r"gho_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}"),
   429	        ("Private Key", r"-----BEGIN (RSA |EC )?PRIVATE KEY-----"),
   430	        ("Anthropic API Key", r"sk-ant-[a-zA-Z0-9\-]{20,}"),
   431	        ("AWS Secret", r"(?i)aws_secret_access_key\s*=\s*\S+"),
   432	        (
   433	            "Generic API Key Assignment",
   434	            r"(?i)(api[_-]?key|secret[_-]?key|auth[_-]?token)\s*=\s*['\x22][a-zA-Z0-9]{16,}['\x22]",
   435	        ),
   436	    ]
   437	}
   438	
   439	/// Scan content for potential secrets.
   440	pub fn scan_for_secrets(content: &str) -> Vec<SecretMatch> {
   441	    let patterns = default_secret_patterns();
   442	    let mut matches = Vec::new();
   443	
   444	    for (line_number, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {
   445	        for (name, pattern) in &patterns {
   446	            if let Ok(re) = regex::Regex::new(pattern) {
   447	                if re.is_match(line) {
   448	                    // Redact the matched portion
   449	                    let redacted = re.replace_all(line, "[REDACTED]").to_string();
   450	                    matches.push(SecretMatch {
   451	                        pattern_name: name.to_string(),
   452	                        line_number: line_number + 1,
   453	                        context: redacted,
   454	                    });
   455	                }
   456	            }
   457	        }
   458	    }
   459	
   460	    matches

+tmp_fanout="/tmp/opensession-ledger-fanout.$$"
+: > "$tmp_fanout"
+tmp_notes="/tmp/opensession-notes-fanout.$$"
+: > "$tmp_notes"
 
-echo "=== Pre-push checks passed ==="
+opsession_bin=""
+shim_bin="${HOME}/.local/share/opensession/bin/opensession"
+if [ -x "$shim_bin" ]; then
+    opsession_bin="$shim_bin"
+elif command -v opensession >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+    opsession_bin="$(command -v opensession)"
+fi
+
+fanout_enabled=0
+if [ -n "$opsession_bin" ]; then
+    fanout_enabled=1
+    detected_mode=$("$opsession_bin" setup --print-fanout-mode 2>/dev/null || true)
+    case "$detected_mode" in
+        hidden_ref|git_notes)
+            fanout_mode="$detected_mode"
+            ;;
+    esac
+fi
+
+while read local_ref local_oid remote_ref remote_oid; do
+    # Skip delete pushes
+    if [ "$local_oid" = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ]; then
+        continue
+    fi
+
+    case "$local_ref" in
+        refs/heads/*)
+            if [ "$fanout_enabled" -eq 1 ]; then
+                branch="${local_ref#refs/heads/}"
+                "$opsession_bin" setup --sync-branch-session "$branch" --sync-branch-commit "$local_oid" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
+                ledger_ref=$("$opsession_bin" setup --print-ledger-ref "$branch" 2>/dev/null || true)
+                if [ -n "$ledger_ref" ]; then
+                    printf '%s\n' "$ledger_ref" >> "$tmp_fanout"
+                fi
+                if [ "$fanout_mode" = "git_notes" ]; then
+                    printf '%s\t%s\t%s\n' "$branch" "$local_ref" "$local_oid" >> "$tmp_notes"
+                fi
+            fi
+            ;;
+    esac
+
+    # Determine the range to check
+    if [ "$remote_oid" = "0000000000000000000000000000000000000000" ]; then
+        # New branch — check all commits
+        range="$local_oid"
+    else
+        range="$remote_oid..$local_oid"
+    fi
+
+    # Check diff content for secret patterns
+    git diff "$range" -- 2>/dev/null | grep -nE \
+        '(sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}|ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}|gho_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}|-----BEGIN (RSA |EC )?PRIVATE KEY-----|ANTHROPIC_API_KEY\s*=|OPENAI_API_KEY\s*=|AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY\s*=|DATABASE_URL\s*=.*password)' \
+        > /tmp/opensession-secret-scan.$$ 2>/dev/null
+
+    if [ -s /tmp/opensession-secret-scan.$$ ]; then
+        found_secrets=1
+        printf "${RED}[opensession]${NC} Potential secrets detected in push to ${remote}:\n"
+        echo ""
+        while IFS= read -r line; do
+            printf "  ${YELLOW}!${NC}  %s\n" "$line"
+        done < /tmp/opensession-secret-scan.$$
+        echo ""
+    fi
+
+    rm -f /tmp/opensession-secret-scan.$$
+done
+
+if [ "$fanout_enabled" -eq 0 ]; then
+    printf "${YELLOW}[opensession]${NC} Warning: opensession CLI/shim not found; skipping ledger fanout.\n"
+    if [ "$strict_mode" -eq 1 ]; then
+        printf "${RED}[opensession]${NC} Error: OPENSESSION_STRICT=1 and fanout helper is unavailable.\n"
+        exit 1
+    fi
+else
+    tmp_sorted="${tmp_fanout}.sorted"
+    sort -u "$tmp_fanout" > "$tmp_sorted" 2>/dev/null
+    while IFS= read -r ledger_ref; do
+        [ -z "$ledger_ref" ] && continue
+        # No local ledger yet for this branch; skip silently.
+        if ! git show-ref --verify --quiet "$ledger_ref"; then
+            continue
+        fi

- [`.githooks/pre-push.pre-opensession`](~/opensession/.githooks/pre-push.pre-opensession)는 그 과정에서 생성된 원본 보존 파일(로컬 산출물)입니다.
